methods.description: Field sampling was conducted to collect soil and vegetation samples from various sites in the Oryol region during the summers of 2016 and 2017. This method is used to gather data for analyzing the transition coefficient of Cs-137 to plants and to measure exposure dose rates.

steps.description: Field sampling was carried out in the summer (July 2016 and 2017), in operating or abandoned farms of settlements of the Oryol region.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field sampling in Oryol region farms

steps.description: The location of the sampling site in 2006 was established using GPS Garmin 62stc.
steps.searchable_summary: establish sampling site location using GPS

steps.description: Soil sampling was carried out at 3 (2016) or 5 (2017) points (envelope method or diagonal 'envelope': a point in the center and two in the upper and lower corners of the site).
steps.searchable_summary: perform soil sampling using envelope method

steps.description: At each point, the der (exposure dose rate) was measured at a height of 10 cm and 1 m from the soil surface, at the sampling points.
steps.searchable_summary: measure exposure dose rate at specified heights

steps.description: Soil samples were taken in 2016, in layers every five centimeters, as in 2006, in three of the five points of each site, diagonally.
steps.searchable_summary: collect soil samples in 5 cm layers diagonally

steps.description: For soil sampling, sections of 20x50 cm in size and 50 cm in depth were laid and samples were taken along the front wall by a ring with a diameter of 20 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: lay sections and collect soil samples using a ring

steps.description: In 2017, samples were taken with a shovel from the selected genetic horizons of the laid profiles and layered-upper 0-10 cm and a layer of 20-30 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect soil samples from genetic horizons using a shovel

steps.description: Simultaneously with soil samples, vegetation samples (leaves, grass, cereals, needles, cones) were taken to determine the transition coefficient (CP) Cs-137 to plants.
steps.searchable_summary: collect vegetation samples for Cs-137 transition coefficient

steps.description: Soil samples were taken in accordance with GOST 17.4.3.01-83, by the 'envelope' method with a side of 100 m, a sampler with a diameter of 20 cm to a depth of 0-0.1 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect soil samples using GOST 17.4.3.01-83 standards

steps.description: The power of the exposure dose was measured at a height of 0.10 m and 1 m from the ground surface, 5 measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: measure exposure dose power at specified heights

steps.description: Samples of vegetation were selected in accordance with GOST 27262-87 'Feed of plant origin', on 8 plots of 1-3 m2, located diagonally.
steps.searchable_summary: select vegetation samples using GOST 27262-87 standards

steps.description: From each plot were selected 300-500 g were prepared and combined sample, from which were selected the average sample weighing 1 to 1.5 kg.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and combine vegetation samples for analysis
